message.content:
Fix BCG yawp-capture violations in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh`.

The problem: lines use `buyy_ui_yawp "X"; buh_line "... ${z_buym_yelp} ..."` — consuming the transient yawp slot directly instead of capturing to a `local -r` variable first.

BCG contract: "Caller MUST capture to `local -r` on the same line — semicolon-separated"

The fix pattern:
```bash
# BEFORE (wrong):
buyy_ui_yawp "Choose an account"; buh_line "  1. ${z_buym_yelp} - Select the Google account for this payor"

# AFTER (correct):
buyy_ui_yawp "Choose an account"; local -r z_choose="${z_buym_yelp}"
buh_line "  1. ${z_choose} - Select the Google account for this payor"
```

There are 4 instances, all in the same function, around lines 449-453. Variable names must be unique within the function scope since they're `local -r`.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT use `cd`. Use absolute paths.

Read the file first (it's large — focus on the area around lines 440-460), then make all 4 edits.

content.text: Let me read the relevant section of the file first.

content.content:
435	  buc_step 'Check existing credentials'
436	  local -r z_rbro_file="${RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE}"
437	  if test -f "${z_rbro_file}"; then
438	    buc_log_args "Existing RBRO credentials will be replaced"
439	  fi
440	
441	  local z_refresh_token=""
442	  buc_step 'OAuth authorization flow'
443	  local -r z_auth_url="https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth?client_id=${z_client_id}&redirect_uri=urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob&scope=openid%20email%20https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform%20https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-billing&response_type=code&access_type=offline"
444	
445	  buh_e
446	  buh_link "Open this URL in your browser: " "Google OAuth Authorization" "${z_auth_url}"
447	  buh_e
448	  buh_line "You will see three or four screens:"
449	  buyy_ui_yawp "Choose an account"; buh_line "  1. ${z_buym_yelp} - Select the Google account for this payor"
450	  buyy_ui_yawp "Google hasn't verified this app"; buh_line "  2. If screen says ${z_buym_yelp}, click Continue"
451	  buh_line "     Otherwise, proceed to next step"
452	  buyy_ui_yawp "Recipe Bottle Payor wants access"; buh_line "  3. ${z_buym_yelp} - Review the requested permissions"
453	  buyy_ui_yawp "Continue"; buh_line "     Check the permission checkboxes to grant access, then click ${z_buym_yelp}"
454	  buh_line "  4. Authorization code will be displayed"
455	  buh_e
456	  local z_auth_code
457	  z_auth_code=$(buh_prompt "Copy the authorization code and paste here: ")
458	  test -n "${z_auth_code}" || buc_die "Authorization code is required"
459	
460	  buc_log_args "Exchanging authorization code for tokens"
461	
462	  # Build request and pipe to curl - secrets never touch disk
463	  local z_response
464	  z_response=$(jq -n \
465	    --arg code "${z_auth_code}" \
466	    --arg client_id "${z_client_id}" \
467	    --arg client_secret "${z_client_secret}" \
468	    --arg redirect_uri "urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob" \
469	    --arg grant_type "authorization_code" \

content.text: Now I have the 4 violations clearly. Let me make all 4 edits in one operation.

input.old_string:
buyy_ui_yawp "Choose an account"; buh_line "  1. ${z_buym_yelp} - Select the Google account for this payor"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Google hasn't verified this app"; buh_line "  2. If screen says ${z_buym_yelp}, click Continue"
  buh_line "     Otherwise, proceed to next step"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Recipe Bottle Payor wants access"; buh_line "  3. ${z_buym_yelp} - Review the requested permissions"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Continue"; buh_line "     Check the permission checkboxes to grant access, then click ${z_buym_yelp}"
input.new_string:
buyy_ui_yawp "Choose an account"; local -r z_yelp_choose="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "  1. ${z_yelp_choose} - Select the Google account for this payor"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Google hasn't verified this app"; local -r z_yelp_unverified="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "  2. If screen says ${z_yelp_unverified}, click Continue"
  buh_line "     Otherwise, proceed to next step"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Recipe Bottle Payor wants access"; local -r z_yelp_access="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "  3. ${z_yelp_access} - Review the requested permissions"
  buyy_ui_yawp "Continue"; local -r z_yelp_continue="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buh_line "     Check the permission checkboxes to grant access, then click ${z_yelp_continue}"

content.content: The file /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
All 4 violations fixed in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh`, lines 449-453.

Each `buyy_ui_yawp` call now immediately captures `${z_buym_yelp}` to a `local -r` variable on the same semicolon-separated line, and the subsequent `buh_line` call references the captured variable instead of the transient slot:

- `z_yelp_choose` — "Choose an account"
- `z_yelp_unverified` — "Google hasn't verified this app"
- `z_yelp_access` — "Recipe Bottle Payor wants access"
- `z_yelp_continue` — "Continue"

All four variable names are unique within the function scope, satisfying the `local -r` constraint.
